subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **Game board** (fixed map of hexagonal terrain spaces in 7 types: Plains, Swamp, Lakes, Forest, Mountains, Wasteland, Desert)
- **14 faction boards** (each faction tied to a specific terrain type, with unique abilities and building tracks)
- **Wooden structures** per faction: Dwellings, Trading Houses, Temples, Stronghold, Sanctuary
- **Workers** (brown cubes), **Coins** (gold tokens), **Priests** (cylindrical tokens in player color)
- **Power tokens** (purple tokens cycling through 3 bowls on faction board)
- **Cult board** (4 cult tracks: Fire, Water, Earth, Air)
- **Scoring tiles** (1 per round, 6 total)
- **Bonus tiles** (selected at round start)
- **Favor tiles** (gained from Temples and Sanctuary)
- **Town tiles** (formed when buildings cluster together with enough power value)
- **Action tokens** (for power actions and special actions)
- **Bridge pieces** (allow non-adjacent connections across rivers)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Place the **game board** in the center — it shows a fixed map of terrain hexes.
2. Place the **Cult board** nearby with all four cult tracks visible.
3. Shuffle **Scoring tiles** and place one per round slot (6 total). These determine round-specific bonuses for certain actions.
4. Place all **Bonus tiles** face-up near the board (number depends on player count + 3).
5. Lay out **Favor tiles**, **Town tiles**, and action space tokens.
6. Place Workers, Coins, and Priests in a general supply.
7. Set the round marker to Round 1.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player selects a **faction**. Each faction is tied to one of 7 terrain types and has unique special abilities, starting resources, and building upgrade costs. The 14 factions (2 per terrain) include:
   - **Plains**: Halflings, Cultists
   - **Swamp**: Alchemists, Darklings
   - **Lakes**: Mermaids, Swarmlings
   - **Forest**: Witches, Auren
   - **Mountains**: Dwarves, Engineers
   - **Wasteland**: Nomads, Chaos Magicians
   - **Desert**: Fakirs, Giants
2. Take your faction board and place all structures on their designated building track slots. Structures cover resource income values — as you build and place structures on the map, you uncover higher income.
3. Set up your **Power bowls** (Bowl I, II, III) with starting power tokens as shown on your faction board.
4. Take starting resources (Workers, Coins, Priests) as shown.
5. In reverse player order, each player places **2 starting Dwellings** on the map on hexes of their native terrain type.
6. Each player selects a **Bonus tile** for Round 1 (in reverse player order).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Terra Mystica is a strategy game of competing factions building settlements on a shared map. Each faction can only build on its **native terrain type**, so you must **terraform** other terrain before building. The map is tight and contested — building adjacent to opponents is both a threat and an opportunity (you can leech power from their building actions).

Over **6 rounds**, players expand their territory, upgrade buildings to unlock abilities and increase income, advance on cult tracks for bonus VP, and form Towns (clusters of buildings) for powerful Town tiles.

There is **no luck** — no dice, no card draws during play. It is a pure strategy game of resource management, spatial planning, and timing.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each round follows this structure:

### 1. Income Phase
All players simultaneously collect income based on their faction board:
- **Workers**: From Dwellings (more Dwellings built = more Workers uncovered).
- **Coins**: From Trading Houses.
- **Priests**: From Temples and Sanctuary.
- **Power**: From certain structures and Favor tiles.
- **Bonus tile income**: Your current Bonus tile may provide additional resources.

### 2. Action Phase
Players take turns performing **one action** each, going clockwise. After completing an action, play passes to the next player. Continue until all players have passed.

Available actions:

#### a) Transform and Build
- **Terraform** a hex adjacent to one of your existing structures. Each terrain type is arranged on a transformation wheel — the number of "steps" between your native terrain and the target terrain determines the Worker cost.
- After terraforming to your native terrain, you may immediately **build a Dwelling** on that hex (costs Workers + Coins).

#### b) Upgrade a Structure
Upgrade an existing structure on the map:
- **Dwelling → Trading House** (costs Workers + Coins): Unlocks coin income.
- **Trading House → Temple** (costs Workers + Coins): Unlocks a Priest income slot and grants a **Favor tile** (permanent bonus from cult advancement or resources).
- **Trading House → Stronghold** (costs Workers + Coins): Unique per faction — unlocks your faction's **special ability**.
- **Temple → Sanctuary** (costs Workers + Coins): Grants another Favor tile and Priest income.

#### c) Send a Priest to a Cult Track
Place a Priest on one of the 4 cult tracks (Fire, Water, Earth, Air) to advance your position. Higher positions yield more VP at game end and during certain scoring rounds.

#### d) Power Actions
Spend Power from Bowl III to perform special actions:
- Gain Workers, Coins, or Priests.
- Terraform 1 step for free (a spade action).
- Build a Bridge.
- Gain 7 VP.
Each Power Action space can only be used **once per round** (across all players).

#### e) Special Actions
Some Bonus tiles, Favor tiles, and faction abilities grant special actions (marked with a unique icon). Each can be used once per round.

#### f) Pass
When you pass, you return your current Bonus tile and select a **new Bonus tile** for next round. Early passers get first pick of Bonus tiles. You take no more actions this round.

### 3. Cult Bonuses and Scoring Tile
After all players pass:
- Resolve the **Scoring tile** for this round (e.g., "2 VP per Dwelling built this round" or "3 VP per Trading House built").
- Advance the round marker.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Terraforming
The **terrain transformation wheel** determines terraforming costs:
- Terrain types are arranged in a circle: Plains → Swamp → Lakes → Forest → Mountains → Wasteland → Desert → Plains.
- To terraform, count the minimum steps between the target terrain and your native terrain on the wheel.
- Each step costs **Workers** (typically 3 Workers per step, reduced by faction abilities or the Stronghold).
- Some factions have special terraforming abilities (e.g., Dwarves can tunnel to non-adjacent hexes).

### Power Cycle
Power tokens cycle through **3 Bowls**:
- **Bowl I → Bowl II → Bowl III**: When you gain Power, move tokens from I to II, then II to III.
- **Bowl III is spendable**: Spend Power from Bowl III for Power Actions. Spent Power goes to Bowl I.
- Gaining Power when all tokens are in Bowl III means wasted Power — manage your cycle.

### Leeching Power
When an opponent builds or upgrades **adjacent to your structure**, you may **leech Power** equal to the structure's power value (Dwelling = 1, Trading House = 2, Temple/Stronghold = 3, Sanctuary = 3).
- Leeching costs **1 VP per Power gained minus 1** (so leeching 3 Power costs 2 VP).
- This is a critical decision — gaining Power is valuable but costs VP.

### Towns
When you have a **connected cluster of structures** with a total **Power value of 7 or more**, you form a **Town**:
- Take a **Town tile** granting a one-time bonus (VP, resources, or advancement) and ongoing benefits.
- Towns are marked on the board and contribute to end-game scoring.
- Forming Towns is a key scoring milestone.

### Favor Tiles
Gained from building Temples and the Sanctuary:
- Each Favor tile provides a **cult track advancement** and an ongoing bonus (income, VP per action, etc.).
- Favor tiles are limited — choosing the right ones synergizes with your strategy.

### Bridges
Bridges can be built (via Power Action or special ability) to connect non-adjacent hexes across rivers. Bridged hexes count as adjacent for Town formation and other purposes.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Faction Asymmetry
Each of the 14 factions plays very differently:
- **Halflings**: Extra VP for terraforming. Excellent at aggressive expansion.
- **Darklings**: Convert Priests into Workers for terraforming — priest-hungry but efficient.
- **Mermaids**: Can skip water hexes for town adjacency. Town-focused.
- **Witches**: Place a free Dwelling on Forest when founding a Town. Fast expansion.
- **Dwarves**: Can tunnel to terraform non-adjacent hexes. Flexible placement.
- **Engineers**: Reduced Bridge costs; bridges count for Town connection.
- **Nomads**: Start with 3 Dwellings. Strong early economy.
- **Chaos Magicians**: Only 1 starting Dwelling but extremely powerful Stronghold (double-action turns).
- **Alchemists**: Convert Coins to VP or VP to Coins at will. Economic flexibility.
- Each faction's Stronghold unlocks a unique power that fundamentally shapes strategy.

### Adjacency
Most actions care about adjacency — you can only terraform and build adjacent to existing structures. This makes initial Dwelling placement critical.

### Shipping
Some factions have a **Shipping value** that extends adjacency across rivers/water. Increasing Shipping (via research or abilities) lets you reach distant hexes as if adjacent.

### Player Interaction
Interaction is constant:
- **Spatial competition**: Contested terrain, racing for key hexes.
- **Power leeching**: Building near opponents gives them Power (deliberate or unavoidable).
- **Action space competition**: Power Actions and some special actions are first-come-first-served.
- **Cult track competition**: Limited top positions on cult tracks.
- **Bonus tile drafting**: Passing order determines Bonus tile selection.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
After **Round 6**, final scoring occurs:

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **In-game VP** | All VP earned during the 6 rounds (from building, scoring tiles, power actions, favor tiles, etc.) |
| **Cult Track Scoring** | For each of the 4 cult tracks, players earn VP based on their position: 8 VP for 1st, 4 VP for 2nd, 2 VP for 3rd. Reaching the top position (10) during the game grants a one-time bonus (usually a Town tile). |
| **Largest Connected Network** | The player with the most structures in a single connected group scores bonus VP. Scoring is tiered: 1st place = 18 VP, 2nd = 12 VP, 3rd = 6 VP. |

The player with the **most total VP** wins. Ties are broken by most resources remaining (Workers + Coins + Power in Bowl III).

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Plan your starting Dwellings carefully**. Adjacency is everything — place where you have room to expand toward your goals.
- **Build near opponents deliberately**. Leeching Power is important, even if it costs a few VP. The Power is usually worth more.
- **Focus on 2-3 Cult tracks**, not all 4. Spreading thin means scoring nothing.
- **Form Towns as soon as possible**. Town tiles are powerful and limited.
- **Watch the Scoring tiles**. If this round rewards Trading House building, build Trading Houses. Align your actions with the round bonus.
- **Don't hoard resources**. Unspent resources at game end are worth very little. Invest everything.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Round scoring alignment**: Plan your 6 rounds around the Scoring tiles. If round 3 rewards Dwellings, save your Dwelling-building for round 3.
- **Town rush**: Forming Towns early gets you the best Town tiles and unlocks scoring. Plan your building clusters to hit 7 power value efficiently.
- **Network planning**: The Largest Connected Network bonus (up to 18 VP) is massive. Plan a contiguous sprawl from the start. Don't split your settlements.
- **Faction-specific mastery**: Each faction has an optimal strategy. Learn your faction's Stronghold power and build your entire game around it.
- **Power cycle management**: Keep your Power cycling efficiently. Don't let tokens pile up in Bowl I. Leech aggressively when the Power will flow through to Bowl III quickly.
- **Tempo and passing**: Passing early lets you pick the best Bonus tile, but passing late lets you take more actions. Balance based on what Bonus tiles you need.
- **Cult positioning**: Reaching position 10 on a cult track first grants a one-time bonus (often a Town). Race for key cult positions when it aligns with your Favor tiles.
- **Opponent awareness**: Track what your opponents need. If they want a specific hex, consider taking it first. If they need a Power Action, use it before them.
